comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: REUSE (op-cert drift, 2026-07-13): era-scoped key validity is already shipped in src/rebar/attest/authorship.py (validity as commit-ancestry positions added_at_commit/revoked_at_commit, compared via 'git merge-base --is-ancestor'; verdict key_not_valid_at_era is in schemas/verify_signature_result.schema.json). Re-scope this task to REUSE that mechanism for the environment keyring, not build a parallel one — likely a thin adapter. Consider folding into child 368c if little unique work remains. See proposal comment on epic sonic-columned-sturgeon.

compiled_state.description:
## Context
Environment signing keys rotate using era-scoped validity (valid-at-log-position, not wall-clock) so past certificates keep verifying and rotated-out keys cannot sign new ones.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Env-key rotation uses era-scoped validity; a cert from a key valid at its era verifies while one past its rotation does not (test).
- [ ] Rotation is documented; no trusted-clock dependency.
compiled_state.title: op-cert: environment-key era-scoped rotation
